skill.name: skill-developer
skill.description: Guide for creating, configuring, testing, and troubleshooting Claude Code skills, including trigger rules, hook behavior, skip controls, and progressive disclosure.
skill.summary: Build Claude Code skills with structured triggers, hook guidance, and practical troubleshooting steps.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command execution, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but review shows these are Markdown examples and reference text rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration, malicious network call, or bundled script was found. The skill is publishable with a warning because it teaches hook patterns that can inject context into Claude and block tool use if implemented unsafely.

medium_findings.title: Security-Sensitive Hook Control Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill documents hooks that inject stdout into Claude context and hooks that block tool use through stderr and exit code 2. This is legitimate skill-development guidance, but copied implementations must sanitize inputs and keep hook messages trusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly describe context injection and blocking hook behavior. The content is documentation rather than executable code, so the risk is conditional on user implementation.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The detected shell commands are examples for testing, validating, and troubleshooting hooks. They appear inside Markdown code blocks and are not shipped as runnable hook scripts in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All sampled command detections are fenced examples or troubleshooting snippets. The repository contains Markdown files only, so no active command execution path was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto detections appear to match ordinary words and documentation text such as description or describe, not cryptographic APIs or hash algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose and schema tables, not encryption calls. No evidence of MD5, DES, SHA1, or other weak crypto implementation was found in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Are Benign References
low_findings.description: The detected URLs point to regex101.com for manual regex testing. No code sends data to these URLs and no telemetry behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation references. They are not part of a network request implementation or an automated exfiltration path.

low_findings.title: Filesystem and Credential Findings Are Configuration Documentation
low_findings.description: Hidden .claude paths, state files, and keyword references are documentation for Claude Code configuration. The certificate or key-file finding is a false positive on trigger keyword text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss skill paths and the word keywords. They do not read secrets, open key files, or access hidden files at runtime.

dangerous_patterns.title: Security-Sensitive Hook Control Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents hooks that inject stdout into Claude context and hooks that block tool use through stderr and exit code 2. This is legitimate skill-development guidance, but copied implementations must sanitize inputs and keep hook messages trusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly describe context injection and blocking hook behavior. The content is documentation rather than executable code, so the risk is conditional on user implementation.

content.value_statement: Creating reliable Claude Code skills requires clear triggers, hooks, and validation rules. This guide helps teams design, test, and troubleshoot skills with structured reference material.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to structure SKILL.md files with frontmatter and focused reference files.
Documents keyword, intent, file path, and content trigger patterns for skill activation.
Describes UserPromptSubmit and PreToolUse hook behavior for suggestions and guardrails.
Provides troubleshooting steps for trigger failures, false positives, and hook execution issues.
Includes pattern examples for database, frontend, backend, workflow, and testing scenarios.
Covers skip markers, session state, environment overrides, and validation checklists.
limitations: It does not include runnable hook source files or an installer.
It focuses on Claude Code skill workflows, not every AI agent framework.
Examples require adaptation before use in production repositories.
Future enhancement sections describe ideas, not implemented features.

use_cases.title: Create a Team Skill
use_cases.description: Define a new Claude Code skill with clear activation language, references, and testing steps.

use_cases.title: Tune Activation Rules
use_cases.description: Improve keyword, regex, path, and content patterns to reduce missed activations and false positives.

use_cases.title: Debug Hook Behavior
use_cases.description: Diagnose why a skill suggestion, guardrail, or hook command is not working as expected.

prompt_templates.title: Begin a New Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Claude Code skill for [topic]. Define the SKILL.md structure, trigger keywords, and three realistic test prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Triggers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these skill trigger rules for false positives and false negatives. Suggest safer keyword, intent, file path, and content patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Guardrail
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a blocking guardrail skill for [risk]. Include when it should trigger, when it should not trigger, and what the block message should require.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Skill System
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Claude Code skill setup for activation quality, hook safety, skip controls, performance, and documentation gaps.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for reviewing database migrations before edits.
output: A concise skill purpose and activation description.
Recommended keyword and intent triggers for migration work.
A guardrail flow with validation steps and safe skip conditions.

output_examples.input: My skill does not activate when I ask about frontend components.
output: A checklist covering skill names, JSON syntax, keyword coverage, and hook registration.
Suggestions for broader but still specific trigger patterns.
Manual test steps to confirm whether the hook sees the expected prompt.

output_examples.input: Reduce false positives from a broad content pattern.
output: An explanation of why the current pattern matches unrelated files.
More specific import, method, or path-based alternatives.
A testing plan using real prompts and representative file paths.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md focused and move detailed references into one level of linked files.
Test triggers with realistic prompts, file paths, and file contents before publishing.
Use blocking guardrails only for high-impact errors with clear recovery steps.
anti_patterns: Do not use broad keywords that activate on common words without domain context.
Do not rely on skip markers or environment overrides as normal workflow controls.
Do not copy hook examples into production without reviewing input handling and trust boundaries.

faq.question: Does this skill create skills automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance, templates, and troubleshooting steps. The user or agent still creates the files and rules.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The content is most specific to Claude Code skill workflows.

faq.question: Does it include executable hooks?
faq.answer: No. It documents hook behavior and test commands, but the reviewed package contains Markdown guidance files only.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill is publishable, but it teaches hook patterns that can inject context or block tools if implemented unsafely.

faq.question: Can I use it for guardrail skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains blocking guardrails, block messages, session tracking, file markers, and environment overrides.

faq.question: What should I review before using it?
faq.answer: Review hook trust boundaries, trigger scope, skip controls, and all command examples before adapting them to a project.
